title: Getting Started
content: This document describes the steps to prepare the necessary tools, clone the project, configure the project for the first time, and run the application on both iOS and Android emulators.

title: Prerequisites
content: Before getting started, please verify that the following tools are installed.

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#p0
title: Flutter and Dart

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#p1
title: Flutter and Dart
content: This project uses FVM (Flutter Version Management) to standardize Flutter version control within the team and reduce version incompatibility issues between team members.

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#p2
title: Flutter and Dart
content: Please install FVM by following the instructions at this link according to your operating system (macOS, Linux, or Windows).

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#p3
title: Flutter and Dart
content: Once FVM is installed, use the command below to download the correct Flutter version:

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#p4
title: Flutter and Dart
content: After installing FVM, to use the fvm command from anywhere in the terminal, you need to configure PATH as follows:

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#l5-i0
title: Flutter and Dart
content: For macOS / Linux: Add the following line to your ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, or the config file for your shell

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#l5-i1
title: Flutter and Dart
content: After setting PATH, run source ~/.bashrc or source ~/.zshrc, or restart the terminal for the changes to take effect.

id: Getting Started > Prerequisites > Flutter and Dart#l5-i2
title: Flutter and Dart
content: For Windows: Add %LOCALAPPDATA%PubCachebin to your computer's Environment Variables.
